weatherForecast.comment: In the night to Sunday it will be cloudy and it will start to snow in the northern Alps. The wind picks up during the night and the snow showers spread to the Niedere Tauern. There will be hardly any snow further south. On Sunday, dense clouds will build up from the north until midday on the northern side of the Alps. There will be light snowfall with limited visibility, especially in the morning. The weather will improve in the afternoon. On the southern side of the Alps (Turracher Höhe), the north foehn will ensure sunny conditions from midday at the latest. There will be a strong northerly wind, even gale force in exposed areas. This will subside in the afternoon. It has cooled down considerably: the temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres is between -9 and -3 degrees. Strong winds with gusts of around 50 km/h from the west. The snowfall level is between the valleys and around 1100 metres above sea level. Amounts of new snow of up to 10 cm are expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New fallen snow and wind slabs increase the avalanche danger!
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche danger increases rapidly with altitude and is rated as considerable above approx. 1800 metres. The avalanche situation remains very delicate for alpinists. The persistent weak layer problem is still present and difficult to assess. In addition, fresh wind slab leads to an increase in avalanche danger. The avalanche prone locations are from north-west to east in terrain adjacent to ridgelines and at the entrances to gullies and bowls.  Medium-sized slab avalanches can be triggered by individuals. It is very likely that avalanches will tear through to deeper layers.

snowpackStructure.comment: Fresh wind slab overlays soft layers of faceted crystals and surface hoar. The persistent weak layer is weakened by the kinetic metamorphism. The wind slab falls cold and is brittle.

tendency.highlights: Strong to stormy westerly winds will blow across the northern side of the Alps on Monday night as a warm front passes through. A few centimetres of new fallen snow may fall in the northern Limestone Alps. In the evening and during the night to Tuesday, a north-westerly weather pattern will set in, which will lead to renewed snowfall in the northern foothills. The considerable risk of avalanches remains.

weatherForecast.comment: The south-westerly wind will pick up in the first half of the night and will be strong to stormy in the morning. On the northern side of the Alps, the peaks will be covered in clouds in the morning and there will be some new fallen snow. In the afternoon, sunny spells will clear up. It will be warmer at all altitudes. At 2000 metres, temperatures will rise from -10 to -4 degrees, at 1500 metres from -8 to -1 degrees. The snowfall level will be between low altitudes and 800 metres. There will be strong winds with gusts of around 50 km/h from south to west. The snowfall level is in the valleys. Amounts of new snow of up to 10 cm are expected.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is still moderate above around 1900 metres and low below. There is still a persistent weak layer problem. In addition, fresh wind slab is to be expected! The avalanche prone locations are limited but still difficult to recognise. The avalanche prone locations are mainly on north to east facing slopes above 1900 metres. Older wind slab snow is in the northern sector, fresh wind slab snow in the eastern sector. Slab avalanches can be triggered by individuals in a few places here. The slab can tear through to the layer close to the ground.

snowpackStructure.comment: The persistent weak layer problem still exists. Surface hoar, angular forms or a hard old snowpack have been covered. In the northern and eastern exposures, the old snowpack continues to be weakened by crusts and layers with faceted crystals and floating snow.

tendency.highlights: Strong to stormy westerly winds will blow across the northern side of the Alps on Monday night as a warm front passes through. A few centimetres of new fallen snow may fall in the northern Limestone Alps. Monday will be mostly free of precipitation during the day and the clouds will soften on the southern side of the Alps. In the evening and during the night on Tuesday, a north-westerly weather pattern will set in, resulting in renewed snowfall in the northern foothills. No significant change in the avalanche risk is expected.

weatherForecast.comment: In the night to Sunday it will be cloudy and it will start to snow in the northern Alps. The wind picks up during the night and the snow showers spread to the Niedere Tauern. There will be hardly any snow further south. On Sunday, dense clouds will build up from the north until midday on the northern side of the Alps. There will be light snowfall with limited visibility, especially in the morning. The weather will improve in the afternoon. On the southern side of the Alps, the northerly foehn will ensure sunny conditions from midday at the latest. There will be strong northerly winds, even gale force in exposed areas. This will ease in the afternoon. It has cooled down considerably: between -7 and -11 degrees are expected at an altitude of 2000 metres by midday. In the Hochschwab area, amounts of new snow of up to 10 cm are expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger, but persistent weak layer problems in the northern and eastern exposures!
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. In the northern and eastern exposures, there is still a persistent weak layer problem at altitudes above the tree line. In steep terrain, avalanches can be triggered in a few places. Fresh wind slab snow is deposited in the Hochschwab area and can also be triggered as a snow slab. However, even if the old snowpack is torn through, the avalanches usually remain small.

snowpackStructure.comment: Some new fallen snow covers surface hoar or angular shapes on the north side. The foundation is still weak.

tendency.highlights: Strong to stormy westerly winds will blow across the northern side of the Alps on Monday night as a warm front passes through. A few centimetres of new fallen snow may fall in the northern Limestone Alps. Monday will be mostly free of precipitation during the day and the clouds will soften on the southern side of the Alps. In the evening and during the night on Tuesday, a north-westerly weather pattern will set in, resulting in renewed snowfall in the northern foothills. No significant change in the avalanche risk is expected.

weatherForecast.comment: In the evening, the stormy northerly wind weakens and turns to the south-west, with a warm front approaching from the west. In the second half of the night, the south-westerly wind picks up and blows strongly to gale force in the morning. On the northern side of the Alps, the peaks will be covered in clouds in the morning and there will be some new fallen snow. There will be sunny clearing in the afternoon. On the southern side of the Alps, some clouds will pass over the summits, with sunny clear spells in between. It will remain mostly free of precipitation. It will be warmer at all altitudes. At 2000 metres, temperatures will rise from -10 to -4 degrees, at 1500 metres from -8 to -1 degrees. The snowfall level will be between low altitudes and 800 metres.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. Small slab avalanches can be triggered in extremely steep gullies and bowls in isolated places at high altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment: There is still little snow. The snowpack is damp or crusted can form in places. In the higher elevations, angular snow crystals continue to weaken the stability on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The north-westerly weather conditions will continue on Tuesday. There will be no precipitation on the southern side of the Alps. The wind will remain strong to stormy. No significant change in the avalanche danger.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: South-westerly wind during the day - in the afternoon the wind turns to the north-west!
